agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement notification preferences for users. Bird provided two submissions; v2 supersedes v1 after legal review. The key change: transactional email opt-out was originally an AC but has been removed entirely — transactional emails cannot be disabled. MJ's architecture aligns with Bird v2. Shaq should implement against v2 only.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to set transactional_emails_enabled to false must be rejected with HTTP 403 and error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED"},{"rule":"Marketing emails can be freely toggled by the user","testable_assertion":"A user can set marketing_emails_enabled to true or false and the change persists"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences is the single endpoint for preference changes","implementation_note":"Partial update semantics — only fields present in the request body are modified"},{"decision":"marketing_emails_enabled stored as boolean","implementation_note":"Defaults to true for new users (opt-out model, not opt-in)"},{"decision":"transactional_emails_enabled must be validated server-side","implementation_note":"Even though the field exists in the model, the API must reject any request that sets it to false. Return 403 with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. Do NOT rely on client-side enforcement alone."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"User can opt out of marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = true","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as false"},{"criterion":"User can opt back in to marketing emails","given":"An authenticated user with marketing_emails_enabled = false","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"marketing_emails_enabled\": true}","then":"200 OK, marketing_emails_enabled is persisted as true"},{"criterion":"Transactional email opt-out is prohibited","given":"An authenticated user","when":"PATCH /users/{id}/notification-preferences with {\"transactional_emails_enabled\": false}","then":"403 Forbidden with error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; no preferences are modified"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Transactional emails","technical_term":"transactional_emails_enabled","definition":"System-initiated emails required for account operations (password resets, security alerts). Legally mandated — cannot be disabled by the user."},{"domain_term":"Marketing emails","technical_term":"marketing_emails_enabled","definition":"Promotional and non-essential communications. User may freely opt out."}],"contradictions_resolved":[{"conflict":"Bird v1 included AC2 allowing transactional email opt-out, while the v1 domain rule said transactional emails cannot be fully disabled. Bird v2 resolved this by removing AC2 entirely after legal review.","resolution":"v2 supersedes v1. There is no transactional opt-out capability. The API must actively reject attempts to disable transactional emails (AC3 in v2). MJ's architecture already reflects this resolution."}],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":95,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clear and legally grounded","Bird v2 and MJ's architecture are fully aligned","Acceptance criteria are unambiguous"],"low_confidence_areas":["Default value for marketing_emails_enabled not explicitly stated by Bird — assumed true (opt-out model)","Whether the PATCH endpoint should silently ignore transactional_emails_enabled=true or only reject false — current spec only addresses the false case"],"assumptions":["Bird v2 is authoritative and fully supersedes v1","marketing_emails_enabled defaults to true for new users","transactional_emails_enabled=true in a PATCH request is a no-op and should not return an error"]}}

Shaq should implement against v2 only.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Transactional emails cannot be disabled under any circumstances (legal requirement)","testa

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the inputs from Bird and MJ for consistency before producing a handoff brief.

Bird has two submissions - v1 and v2. v2 is the latest and supersedes v1. v2 removed AC2 (transactional email opt-out) and added AC3 (403 on attempted disable).

MJ's submission aligns with Bird v2 - 403 on attempting to set transactional_emails_enabled to false.

No contradictions. Bird v2 and MJ are aligned. Let me produce the handoff brief.


Everything checks out - all outputs are consistent.
